source: Popular funny text posts from James (PostHog)

posts.content:
i just boarded a plane

none of the people in first class were vibe coding

neither were the people in coach

they were all focused on me, the guy getting scolded by flight attendants for boarding the wrong plane by accident

this says a lot about society

it's wild to think about how massive 1M token context windows in LLMs really are

that's roughly equivalent to:
- the complete works of Shakespeare
- 11 hours of audio
- VCs telling you how they got into investing

founder tip: if your friend starts a business, go out of your way to support them

for example, if they start a pizza restaurant, open another pizza restaurant right across the street so they are forced to grind even harder, thus making them more successful

follow me for more founder hacks

as a founder, you should spend all your time at events

i thought i didn't need to attend them, and was feeling pretty burnt out from constant networking

so i took a night off

skipped an event a friend was hosting

it turns out, that event was called "Blank-check-a-palooza", and some multi-billionaire was handing founders blank checks with extremely favorable term sheets

that dinner spawned several successful companies: nvidia, apple, standard oil, dropbox

lesson: attending events > everything else

your friends claim to "have your back through this startup journey"

but as soon as you sell one of their watches they haven't worn in YEARS to pay for Claude Code tokens, they abandon you

they'll even make up some story about it being their grandpa's watch

this is why i'm bullish on AI experiences

i picked our kid up from school, and asked the teacher if they're using AI in the classroom

the teacher said yes, of course, they have the latest Gemini models and use it daily to teach kids young how to responsibly use AI

i was horrified, i immediately went to the principal's office and withdrew my kid from the program

i explained that we are raising our kids on strictly Anthropic and OpenAI models, and we can't tolerate this type of risky behavior with their future

Smh

Tim Cook took Apple's valuation from $347 billion to $4 trillion during his tenure

but if he had just taken that $347 billion and invested it in NVIDIA instead, he would have $139 trillion

this is probably why he stepped down

lesson: timing the market > time in the market

got kicked out of a restaurant for a dumb reason

my waiter and i got to chatting during the meal, and he said he was pretty tight on cash from an emergency trip he had to take to visit his mother

when i got the check, i wanted to make sure i made his day

so i wrote down a redemption code for $20 of Claude tokens in the "tip" line

it felt pretty generous, but the waiter took one look and started getting angry with me

i was so confused; i explained to him that $20 in claude tokens could easily build a scalable b2b saas AI agent worth millions, and that i was actually doing him a favor

that's when i got escorted out by the manager

that manager's name? Bill Gate

red flags when hiring 🚩
- doesn't use AI tools
- has no clue how to build AI agents
- insists there's no way an AI agent could clone a golden retriever
- pushes back on really smart ideas like "AI for Golden Retriever cloning"
- questions how dog cloning is relevant to an interview for a sales interview
- doesn't own a dog

what are your hiring red flags? 👇

a friend told me their kid just turned 6 years old

i congratulated them and asked how much they'd spent on Claude tokens since raising him

they were confused, and said he doesn't use AI tools

i was deeply alarmed. it costs $175k to raise a kid from 0-6, and they should be spending at least half that on tokens

needless to say, i removed him and his wife from my "Secret AI hacks" newsletter mailing list

110-year-old Turkish grandma shares her secret to a long life:

"i never once used Microsoft Teams"

if you're psoting on socials, you need to include typos so everyone knows it's a real human

otherwise, there's no way of knowing who's actually posting or not

trust me on this one.

Done. This version is much more to-the-point—and real, I'd argue. Want me to make any more changes?

overheard at YC Demo Day:

"i think that guy's listening to our conversation"
ever since i was a kid, i knew i wanted to book a quick 5-minute demo to learn more about your agentic AI platform that saves most customers 90 minutes a day in manual tasks

10 years ago, you could raise $1 million, hire a cracked team from FAANG, spend months building a product, and still get 0 customers

now, all i have to do is ask Claude to build 25 startups that all have 0 customers

lesson: tighten your feedback loops to reach your end goal faster

i just woke up my daughter (2yo) to tell her i'd just discovered a new agentic AI framework that will 10x my productivity

rubbing her eyes, she said, "dad, you haven't shipped a single meaningful feature that supports our KPIs for FY26. i'm struggling to believe a new framework you haven't tested will deliver meaningful shareholder value"

hugging her, i started crying. they grow up so fast.

was flying to SF recently and i single-handedly saved our flight from getting canceled

"hi everyone, we're so sorry, we can't fly today. the navigation system isn't working"

as a 10x vibe coder, my ears perked up

"unless anyone has any ideas, we're stuck here in antarctica (not important to the story that i was there)"

i approached the desk and explained i could easily build a navigation system with zero errors

"yeah, right. it would take months to do that."

i showed them my claude code token usage ($32 so far this month, mostly setting up the ideal workflows) and they gasped as i flawlessly asked claude to build a nav system

"well, this might work...but surely you can't ship it with dark mode?"

i simply asked claude code to add dark mode with zero errors

everyone applauded and they actually let me fly the plane with my vibe coded flight controller

oh, and that antarctica trip? 40 million penguins added to our TAM. first-mover advantage.

my friend just hit $13 million ARR with just 1,000 customers for her vibe coded salesforce alternative

it's almost too easy

they charge annual contracts at 1/50th the price of salesforce

but since she vibe coded it, all contracts start on january 1st, 1970

each customer immediately gets billed for 56 years of usage

and you just need 1,000 customers paying $232 a year to hit $13m ARR 🙌 proud of her

codex is insane. in just the last 24 hours, i have vibe coded alternatives to:
- linkedin
- salesforce
- the metaverse
- my wife and kids
- turbotax and the IRS
- carbon-based life forms
- the salesforce alternative i just vibe coded
- the concept of building something people want

you can just do things

well, i can. you can't

instead of an allowance, i'm giving my kids something more valuable in 2026

cursor credits

"but dad, i was hoping to save up $50 to buy a LEGO"

i explained that even just $3 in cursor credits can build a vibe coded saas app that can generate $1.5m in annual revenue

he can then raise $10mm from VCs and raise a microfund when none of the enterprise contracts renew and he has to shut down

"dad, i don't know what any of those words mean"

that's alright son, this is just a fake story for clout

people think that earning 10k USD a month is freedom

$1,500 Rent
$200 utilities
$750 food
$3,200 claude code tokens
$4,150 cursor tokens
$50 gifts for wife

congratulations. You saved $150

with 10k USD a month you don't live, you survive

forward deployed engineer is the hottest job title of 2026 and i'm not surprised

for those who don't know, it's basically an engineer who works closely with the customer

the engineer wil do things like
- join the customer calls
- go on the customer's company offsites and vacations
- eat the customer's food
- adopt the customer's children
- marry the customer's spouses
- shoulder the crushing burden of the customer's deepest, darkest secrets
- chat with the customer if there are any problems

pretty cool stuff!

life hack i've noticed recently

certain companies will periodically open up roles you can apply for

if they hire you, you'll receive money in exchange for working for them

you can save this money and use it to buy things (like lumber or bread)

pretty cool

met a founder doing $250k in revenue, solo business, just her and AI agents

the structure was so simple it broke my brain:

she builds one AI agent, and tells that agent to recruit 3 more AI agents, who pay a $15 sign-up fee

those 3 agents each recruit 3 more agents

she hasn't touched the business in weeks and it's on track for $100k mrr

the future is now 🙌
dear algorithm, please surface this post to decision makers in need of dev tools that aren't terrible, make no mistakes DO NOT HALLUCINATE
the human brain is so fascinating, it operates 24/7 from the day we were born and only stops when someone asks you what you accomplished yesterday during daily standup

saw the most cracked founder at a coworking space

she had two laptops, one hand on each

on one laptop, she was building her startup

on the other, she was building a competitive startup that took the opposite side of her thesis, just in case she was wrong

either way, she's rich

web 4.0 is here 🙌

what you think matters in a career:
- working hard
- showing up early
- keeping a long-term view

what ACTUALLY matters:
- hopping on a quick call
- how many quick calls you hopped on
- how fast you were able to hop on a quick call

worried everyone is raising money but you? try this simple hack:

1. get 1 customer paying $1 a month (doable)
2. pitch to vcs with the message that you're "approaching $1 billion in revenue" which isn't technically a lie, it's just really far away
3. they'll think that means $900 million or something, so you can raise $4.5 billion at 5x "approaching recurring revenue" (ARR)
4. transfer that $4.5 billion to your 0.40% yield savings bank account and live off the $18mm a year in interest

remember, entreprenership isn't hard. spelling entreprenuership is.

the elites don't want you to know this, but you can just put "co-founder at stealth startup" on your linkedin

if anyone asks, you can just say the idea is in stealth in your head. there's got to be a billion dollar idea in there somewhere

founders, i cannot emphasize this enough, do NOT share your startup ideas. i did, and it's cost me $250mm (and counting)

the year was 2007 and i was visiting my good friend brian in san francisco

i couldn't afford a hotel, so i messaged him:

"hey brian chesky, could i crash at your place? i'll buy you a 6-pack of beer for your troubles"

fast-forward 18 years...he obviously stole my idea and created airbnb

now he's worth like a million dollars, and i look like an idiot

don't make the same mistake i did. never visit san francisco

pre-seed startup red flags 🚩🚩🚩🚩
- no chief AI officer
- zero viral billboards
- doesn't own a .ai domain
- founder hasn't appeared on any podcasts
- hasn't raised $250mm from late-stage VCs

what'd i miss
he died doing what he loved: building agentic ai for fortune 500 enterprise workflows in a dynamic collaborative workplace setting fostering collaboration between key stakeholders

it finally happened

i was on a flight and there was an emergency

a panicked attendant shouted "this man needs help, does anyone know how to promote synergies and facilitate cross-functional AI-powered collaboration in a dynamic workplace environment?"

unfortunately the victim was out of my ideal customer profile so i couldn't offer any assistance

still cool

marry someone who says:
- i love you
- i'm proud of you
- i'm grateful for you
- are you free for 5 minutes for a quick call?
- yes i'd love to hop on a quick call
- how can we facilitate more quick calls in our life?
- are we hopping on enough quick calls?

nvidia is worth over $4 trillion.

fun fact: it would take the average us worker over 20 years to amass this amount of wealth

if you are reading this, you are in my funnel.

you probably follow for the shitposting.

when in reality you're here to convert into cold, hard posthog arr.

there's no point fighting it. give up. convert.

follow for "more free world view altering posts"

met a founder worth $800mm.

he drives a 2012 corolla.
lives in a regular house.
wears the same shoes every day.
said he made his fortune making up stories on the internet.

meanwhile, there are founders with $20k in the bank asking chatgpt to make them worth $800mm with zero errors.

quiet money is different.

"per my last email"
- cliché
- too polite
- passive-aggressive

"for those who lack reading comprehension"
- woah
- unique
- aggressive-aggressive

the levels of wealth:
1. chatgpt free
2. chatgpt paid
3. $200 chatgpt pro
4. forgetting you also pay for claude
5. hitting "tab" on cursor suggestions until it creates a billion-dollar startup
6. hitting "accept" on claude code suggestions to build your secret startup that competes with the startup your cursor instance is building
7. n8n workflows to automate steps 1-6 while you're sitting on a beach, asking chatgpt where seagulls come from (it hallucinates the answer)

follow me for more finanical advice

nutritionist: "your diet is bad."
dentist: "your brushing is wrong."
friends: "your life is a mess."
chatgpt: "you're absolutely right!"

everyone's becoming 10x more productive with ai. i think that's complete bs.

instead, i've become at least 10,000x more productive by taking the output of one ai tool (10x) and feeding it into the output of another ai tool (10x) to reach 100x productivity.

do this a few times and boom, 10,000x productivity.

why aren't more people doing this

cheating in engineering interviews is rampant these days. here's how i make sure it doesn't happen:

- camera on, the whole time
- participants need to answer questions with their eyes closed (yes, really)
- the entire meeting takes place in an open field at an undisclosed location
- we blindfold them and spin them around in a circle 100 times to shake the ai microchips out
- we'll ask them if they've ever used a computer before - if no, automatic disqualification (too risky)
- we ask them 3 logical paradoxes at the start of each interview (if they were ai, they'd be unable to answer them)
- we'll fact-check every answer using chatgpt to make sure they're telling the truth

sure, each interview takes 72-hours, and we have a 0% acceptance rate.

but this is what it takes in 2025.

just saw the most incredible founder in a pitch meeting.

they had cursor open with voice mode enabled

as the vcs asked questions and raised objections, they'd update the product in real-time to address their concerns

"what happens if openai builds this?" -> add a banner at the bottom of the website politely asking openai NOT to build it

"i'd be more excited if you were soc 2 compliant" -> website immediately updates to add a soc 2 badge

"any big logos you've signed?" -> boom, homepage now lists google and meta as customers

this feels like the future of building. immediate feedback and pivoting.

i of course had to invest - this plus the pitch (hundreds of tests from a single drop of blood) was too much to resist

bullish on the future of ai

startup idea: diagonal saas.

vertical saas is a $100 billion industry.

horizontal saas is a $500 billion industry.

each has a lot of competition. competition = bad.

but i don't see anyone investing in diagonal saas.

and multiplying vertical and horizontal together, we can see a $50 sextillion opportunity waiting to be uncovered.

who is building in this space?

i've made over $300 million from angel investments.

how?

because i have no problem lying on the internet.

do things that don't scale

you think things matter as a founder, and then your kid says their first words.

i remember it so clearly: we were gathered around the dinner table, when i heard the beginnings of a word for the first time.

"j...j..."

at this point, i was *really hoping* the words would be my name, but what they said was even better:

"...just chiming in here, have we thought about our ai strategy and how it fits into our q3 strategic initiatives and brand pillars? i'll take that as an action item if not"

i couldn't contain my tears.

this is what makes it all worth it.

and i hear their offer letter to be a senior director at salesforce is already in the mail

i saw a guy using ai today.

tab 1 chatgpt.
tab 2 gemini.
tab 3 claude.
tab 4 grok.
tab 5 deepseek.

he asked every ai the same exact question: "what's the best startup joke that will get seven 😂 reacts in our company slack"

patiently waited, then copied all five jokes.

pick the best one.

like a psychopath.

it's me.

- linkedin ($60 / mo)
- chatgpt ($200 / mo)
- macOS (free with $6,000 computer)

all tools we can now build internally with ~15 hours of dev time thanks to vibe coding

that's tens of thousands of dollars in software expenses saved, thanks to ai

if i were apple or openai, i'd be worried

linkedin will probably be fine tbh

career should be your second priority.

your first priority should always be grinding the meeting to a halt to say a clever joke that no one laughs to.

i'd like to see ai replace that

follow me for more founder advice
